content.problemStatement:
Chef has two texts, sourceTextsourceTextsourceText and targetTexttargetTexttargetText. Chef wants to transform the sourceTextsourceTextsourceText into the targetTexttargetTexttargetText using the minimum number of operations. The allowed operations are inserting a character, deleting a character, or replacing a character. Help Chef find the minimum number of such operations needed to convert one text into the other.

Chef needs your help to implement a function that calculates this minimum number of edit operations, also known as the edit distance between the two texts.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecalculateMinimumOperationscalculateMinimumOperationscalculateMinimumOperations

Parameters
sourceTextsourceTextsourceText (string): The original text that needs to be transformed.
targetTexttargetTexttargetText (string): The desired text after applying the operations.
Both inputs consist of lowercase and/or uppercase English letters with length constraints typically up to 500 characters.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the minimum number of edit operations.
The output is a non-negative integer indicating the edit distance between the two texts.
This value quantifies the least number of single-character edits needed for conversion.
Constraints
1≤T≤501 \leq   T  \leq 501≤T≤50
0≤∣sourceText∣,∣targetText∣≤5000 \leq |sourceText|, |targetText| \leq 5000≤∣sourceText∣,∣targetText∣≤500
Both sourceTextsourceTextsourceText and targetTexttargetTexttargetText contain only lowercase and uppercase English letters.
Input Format
The first line contains an integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines:

The first line contains the string sourceTextsourceTextsourceText.
The second line contains the string targetTexttargetTexttargetText.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing an integer — the minimum number of operations Chef needs to convert sourceTextsourceTextsourceText into targetTexttargetTexttargetText.
Help Chef by implementing the calculateMinimumOperationscalculateMinimumOperationscalculateMinimumOperations function to find this minimum number of edit operations for each test case.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 50) — the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line is the source string (may be empty). The second line is the target string (may be empty). Both strings contain only uppercase and lowercase English letters and have length at most 500.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of insertions, deletions or replacements required to convert the source string into the target string (the Levenshtein edit distance).

sampleTestCases.input:
4
kitten
sitting
flaw
lawn
abc
abc
intention
execution
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: kitten → sitting needs 3 edits (k→s, e→i, insert g).
Test case 2: flaw → lawn needs 2 edits (delete f, insert n).
Test case 3: abc → abc needs 0 edits.
Test case 4: intention → execution needs 5 edits (delete t, replace i→e, n→x, n→c, insert u).
Test case 1: abcdef → azced needs 3 edits (b→z, delete d, f→d).\nTest case 2: pqrst → pqrstu needs 1 edit (insert u at the end).
Transform Saturday to Sunday with 3 operations (delete a, delete t, replace r with n).

solution.approach:
For each test case we compute the classic Levenshtein edit distance using dynamic programming. Let dp[i][j] be the minimum number of operations to convert the first i characters of the source into the first j characters of the target. The recurrence is:
- dp[0][j] = j (insert j characters)
- dp[i][0] = i (delete i characters)
- If source[i‑1] == target[j‑1] then dp[i][j] = dp[i‑1][j‑1]
- Otherwise dp[i][j] = 1 + min(dp[i‑1][j]   // delete
                         dp[i][j‑1]   // insert
                         dp[i‑1][j‑1] // replace)
We fill the table row by row. The answer is dp[m][n] where m and n are the lengths of the two strings. The algorithm runs in O(m·n) time and O(m·n) memory, which is fine for lengths up to 500.
